User added:
"He said, "Why were you looking for me? Didn't you know that I had to be here, dealing with the things of my Father?" But they had no idea what he was talking about."
‭‭Luke‬ ‭2:49-50‬ ‭MSG‬‬‬‬‬‬

Joseph and Mary lost track of the young Messiah for a few days. They found Him in the temple. God lays two questions on my heart in reading this story of Jesus. The first: Do I seek Jesus with the same urgency that I did looking for my kids when I lost track of them in the grocery store, or park (Yeah, I did. Don't judge.)?And the second question: Where am I looking today to find Jesus about His Father's business?

-Sean Lennon
Interview Text:
Last Story from Kevin: 

Then there was the time I got cancer. It started when I went in to the doctor for a lump on my testicle. They told me it was probably just an inflammation but I left there with a cancer diagnosis. 
I got into my car and cried. But then a voice, inaudible but coming from my right side said, "This will not be the end of you." It was so powerful and real that I believed it. Even after they operated to remove it and it reappeared in my other testicle years later, I was still the person consoling others. I was okay through the whole process because I believed God said that it would not take me.

Among my friends, Kevin has had the most bruised past. Most sinful and unhealthy and closest to death. And he has also had the most miraculous deliverance and interaction with God. Kevin said he felt hesitant to say it like this, but that he was grateful he had been given the "gift of alcoholism." Like the people in Ethiopia, without the desperation and need for help outside of ourselves, we don't rely on faith.

-Raw Spoon (from Miracles in America)
Music Video Instructions: Imagine if God's love for us, and ours for him was like this.
